title: podman 5.0.0 running as root inside systemd-nspawn containers with user namespacing overmounts the system-wide `/run` directory
body:
### Issue Description

I am not sure whether this is technically a supported use-case, however it used to work fine for me: I run a bunch of systemd-nspawn containers with `PrivateUsers=pick` (so that the root user inside is not the real root), and then run Podman as root inside of those containers. This is useful, for example, when I want to run a CI daemon that requires access to a docker-compatible socket without giving it access to any Podman socket on the host system.

However after upgrading to Podman 5.0.0, this seems to no longer work. Running any Podman container gives the error

```
Error: rootless netns: write ref counter: open /run/containers/storage/networks/rootless-netns/ref-count: no such file or directory
```

It looks like the `/run` directory inside the systemd-nspawn container is overmounted by Podman at some point before this error message is printed (did Podman fail / forget to enter the mountns somehow but without printing an error?). This also causes everything else that requires access to `/run` to break as well.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start a `systemd-nspawn` container with `PrivateUsers=pick`
2. Install Podman inside that `systemd-nspawn` container
3. Attempt to run any Podman container inside the `systemd-nspawn` container (e.g. `sudo podman run --rm -it hello-world`)


### Describe the results you received

Podman fails to execute the container with

```
Error: rootless netns: write ref counter: open /run/containers/storage/networks/rootless-netns/ref-count: no such file or directory
```

And the environment inside the `systemd-nspawn` container is broken due to `/run` being overmounted by Podman.

### Describe the results you expected

Podman used to work just fine running as root inside a non-root user namespace.

### podman info output

```


### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Both host and the `systemd-nspawn` container are on the latest Arch Linux packages as of 2024-03-29

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Downgrading to 4.9.3 seems to resolve the issue. Personally I suspect the new `rootlessnetns` module in `common` might have introduced this issue.

It looks like the mount [here](https://github.com/containers/common/blob/main/libnetwork/internal/rootlessnetns/netns_linux.go#L437) is where that `/run` mount came from. But I'm pretty sure this mount should happen in its own mount namespace...?

Ok, it seems that this `setupMounts()` function simply do not work correctly if `podman` is running with a "fake" root account. We can fix the over-mounting by making the entire mount tree private, but then podman network still cannot get set up properly because `/run` is still not valid inside the mount namespace (it is set up for CNI plugins, but podman itself still tries to look for `ref-count` which is present in the *original* `/run` directory, but not in the version set up for the namespace).

On the other hand, do we actually need to call `setupMounts()` here if podman is run with a fake root inside a usernamespace'd container? To me it looks like it is only needed if CNI plugins cannot write directly to the system-wide `/run` directory. Simply deleting `setupMounts()` seems to make everything work for me.
Could this also be a pasta versus slirp4netns issue, you could change containers.conf to user slirp4netns to see if the issue goes away.  slirp4netns was default in 4.9.*, pasta is default in 5.0
To me it feels like `setupMounts()` is not really needed if `/run` and `/var/lib/cni` are already accessible to the user running podman, hence the patch above.

The patch is not a bad idea but really the whole rootless-netns code must be skipped when running as root with CAP_SYS_ADMIN and CAP_NET_ADMIN. Do you have these caps in your nspawn container?

Basically the code today checsk IsRootless() which is not correct as it also counts a userns as rootless, we should check whenever we re-execed into the podman userns or not.
Making sure we make our mounts private is of course also a good idea to avoid this issue of breaking the "host" system accidentally

@Luap99 Yes, and after checking older Podman (which worked inside `systemd-nspawn`) and Docker I think none of them used rootless netns at all, at least `slirp4netns` or `pasta` are not launched. Bridges can be directly created inside `systemd-nspawn`.
@PeterCxy In case you able to compile from source please test if https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22243 works for you.
@Luap99 Seems to work fine for me.
